name: Ascent to Mirador de Castroviejo — circular route through the Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila
name_original: Subida al Mirador de Castroviejo — circular por el Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Hontoria Del Pinar traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range and the nearby Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila. The region features varied terrain, including forested areas, river valleys, and open stretches with views of peaks and natural parks. Road cyclists can expect routes with significant elevation changes, indicative of the mountainous environment. The area offers a network of roads suitable for different cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Hontoria Del Pinar The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to Mirador de Castroviejo — circular route through the Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila, a 54.6 miles (87.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete, featuring significant climbs through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Canales de la Sierra – Neila loop from Hontoria del Pinar, a difficult 79.0 miles (127.2 km) path. This route offers extensive riding through mountainous terrain with substantial elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the San Leonardo de Yagüe – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Pico de las Navas, a 28.5 miles (45.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Hontoria Del Pinar is defined by mountainous ascents, forested sections, and views of natural parks and peaks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels, with more than 50 routes available. The routes in Hontoria Del Pinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hontoria Del Pinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Hontoria Del Pinar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: The region's mountainous terrain suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially on routes with less tree cover, while winters may bring colder conditions and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: Yes, Hontoria Del Pinar offers a few easier road cycling routes. While many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are options available for those looking for a less strenuous ride. You can find routes with moderate difficulty, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Leonardo de Yagüe – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Pico de las Navas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 28.5 miles with manageable climbs.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Hontoria Del Pinar's mountainous environment provides numerous difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canales de la Sierra – Neila loop from Hontoria del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 79.0-mile path with extensive riding through mountainous terrain and substantial elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056036748/ascent-to-mirador-de-castroviejo-circular-route-through-the-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mirador de Castroviejo — circular route through the Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbs through the natural park.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Hontoria Del Pinar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056036748/ascent-to-mirador-de-castroviejo-circular-route-through-the-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mirador de Castroviejo — circular route through the Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canales de la Sierra – Neila loop from Hontoria del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find scenic road cycling routes with viewpoints in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic beauty, with routes offering views of the Sierra de la Demanda and the Parque natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056036748/ascent-to-mirador-de-castroviejo-circular-route-through-the-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Mirador de Castroviejo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads to a prominent viewpoint. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic vistas.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: The routes often pass through or near significant natural landmarks. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Grande (Río Lobos Canyon)&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3999886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye (Natural Rock Arch)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is characterized by forested areas, river valleys, and views of natural parks.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or cultural sites near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to see historical and cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building that can be found in the vicinity of some routes. Additionally, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5939096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt;, an interesting man-made structure.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about road cycling in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Road cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes mountainous ascents, forested sections, and open stretches with views of natural parks and peaks. The network of roads offers options for all ability levels, from moderate to challenging.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop along the road cycling routes near Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for each route, the presence of towns like San Leonardo de Yagüe and Palacios de la Sierra along routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Leonardo de Yagüe – Palacios de la Sierra loop from Espejón&lt;/a&gt; suggests opportunities for refreshments and breaks. It's advisable to check local amenities in these towns before your ride.

Q: Where can I rent a road bike in Hontoria Del Pinar?
A: While komoot focuses on route planning, for bike rentals, it's best to check with local tourism offices or sports shops in Hontoria Del Pinar or nearby larger towns. They can provide information on rental services available in the area.
